review_info.purpose: ChatGPT review before merging into office_drills.json

packs.title: Keigo Production
packs.description: Practice producing humble and honorific verb forms in workplace contexts

stages.prompt: You're telling your manager you'll do the task. Use the humble form of 'to do' (humble form of する).

modelAnswer.japanese: 私がいたします。
modelAnswer.kana: わたしがいたします。
modelAnswer.english: I will do it.

stages.hint: いたす (humble form of する)

stages.prompt: You're introducing yourself to a client. Use the humble form of 'to say/be called' (humble form of 言う).

modelAnswer.japanese: 田中と申します。
modelAnswer.kana: たなかともうします。
modelAnswer.english: I am called Tanaka. / My name is Tanaka.

stages.hint: 申す (humble form of 言う)

stages.prompt: You're telling a client you'll visit their office tomorrow. Use the humble form of 'to go' (humble form of 行く).

modelAnswer.japanese: 明日そちらに参ります。
modelAnswer.kana: あしたそちらにまいります。
modelAnswer.english: I will go there tomorrow.

stages.hint: 参る (humble form of 行く)

stages.prompt: You're reporting to your manager that you looked at the document. Use the humble form of 'to look at/read' (humble form of 見る).

modelAnswer.japanese: ドキュメントを拝見しました。
modelAnswer.kana: ドキュメントをはいけんしました。
modelAnswer.english: I looked at the document.

stages.hint: 拝見する (humble form of 見る)

stages.prompt: You're asking your manager if you can ask them about the schedule. Use the humble form of 'to ask/visit/hear' (multi-purpose humble verb).

modelAnswer.japanese: スケジュールについて伺ってもよろしいでしょうか。
modelAnswer.kana: スケジュールについてうかがってもよろしいでしょうか。
modelAnswer.english: May I ask you about the schedule?

stages.hint: 伺う (humble form of 聞く/訪問する)

packs.title: Keigo Production 2
packs.description: Practice honorific verbs and complex keigo patterns

stages.prompt: You're telling a colleague that the manager is in the office. Use the honorific form of 'to be' (honorific form of いる).

modelAnswer.japanese: 部長はオフィスにいらっしゃいます。
modelAnswer.kana: ぶちょうはオフィスにいらっしゃいます。
modelAnswer.english: The department head is in the office.

stages.hint: いらっしゃる (honorific form of いる/行く/来る)

stages.prompt: You're reporting what the client said in the meeting. Use the honorific form of 'to say' (honorific form of 言う).

modelAnswer.japanese: お客様は了解しましたとおっしゃいました。
modelAnswer.kana: おきゃくさまはりょうかいしましたとおっしゃいました。
modelAnswer.english: The client said they understood.

stages.hint: おっしゃる (honorific form of 言う)

stages.prompt: You're asking if the manager will do the review. Use the honorific form of 'to do' (honorific form of する).

modelAnswer.japanese: 部長がレビューをなさいますか。
modelAnswer.kana: ぶちょうがレビューをなさいますか。
modelAnswer.english: Will the department head do the review?

stages.hint: なさる (honorific form of する)

stages.prompt: You're thanking the manager for giving you advice. Use the honorific form of 'to give' (honorific form of くれる).

modelAnswer.japanese: アドバイスをくださってありがとうございます。
modelAnswer.kana: アドバイスをくださってありがとうございます。
modelAnswer.english: Thank you for giving me advice.

stages.hint: くださる (honorific form of くれる)

stages.prompt: You're asking the client to please look at the document (complex keigo pattern combining honorific + humble).

modelAnswer.japanese: 資料をご覧いただけますでしょうか。
modelAnswer.kana: しりょうをごらんいただけますでしょうか。
modelAnswer.english: Could you please look at the materials?

stages.hint: ご覧いただく (honorific お/ご + verb stem + いただく)

packs.title: Communication & Culture
packs.titleJa: コミュニケーションと文化
packs.description: Practice essential workplace culture concepts and natural communication patterns

stages.prompt: You're explaining the fundamental Japanese business communication principle (report, contact, consult). What is this concept called?

modelAnswer.japanese: 報連相を徹底しています。
modelAnswer.kana: ほうれんそうをてっていしています。
modelAnswer.english: We thoroughly practice hourensou (report, contact, consult).

stages.hint: 報連相 (ほうれんそう) — report, contact, consult

stages.prompt: You're arriving at the office and greeting your colleagues in Japanese. What do you say?

modelAnswer.japanese: おはようございます。今日も挨拶から始めましょう。
modelAnswer.kana: おはようございます。きょうもあいさつからはじめましょう。
modelAnswer.english: Good morning. Let's start today with greetings.

stages.hint: 挨拶 (あいさつ) — greetings

stages.prompt: You're meeting a client for the first time and need to exchange business cards. What is this ritual called?

modelAnswer.japanese: まずは名刺交換をさせていただきます。
modelAnswer.kana: まずはめいしこうかんをさせていただきます。
modelAnswer.english: First, let me exchange business cards with you.

stages.hint: 名刺交換 (めいしこうかん) — business card exchange

stages.prompt: You're softly suggesting a possibility on Slack without being too direct. Use a natural softening word meaning 'could it be that...'

modelAnswer.japanese: もしかして、こちらの理解で合っていますか。
modelAnswer.kana: もしかして、こちらのりかいであっていますか。
modelAnswer.english: Could it be that this understanding is correct?

stages.hint: もしかして — could it be that / by any chance

stages.prompt: You're explaining an important cultural concept: reading the atmosphere and understanding unspoken context. What is this called?

modelAnswer.japanese: 日本では空気を読むことが大切です。
modelAnswer.kana: にほんではくうきをよむことがたいせつです。
modelAnswer.english: In Japan, reading the atmosphere is important.

stages.hint: 空気を読む (くうきをよむ) — to read the room/atmosphere

packs.title: Daily Coordination
packs.description: Practice coordination vocabulary and acknowledgment phrases

stages.prompt: You're asking team members to contact you individually (not in a group). Use the word for 'individual/separately'.

modelAnswer.japanese: 個別にご連絡ください。
modelAnswer.kana: こべつにごれんらくください。
modelAnswer.english: Please contact me individually.

stages.hint: 個別 (こべつ) — individual, separately

stages.prompt: You're asking each team member to check their own assigned section. Use the word meaning 'each person/respectively'.

modelAnswer.japanese: 各自、担当の箇所を確認してください。
modelAnswer.kana: かくじ、たんとうのかしょをかくにんしてください。
modelAnswer.english: Each person, please check your assigned section.

stages.hint: 各自 (かくじ) — each person, everyone

stages.prompt: You're telling a client you need to coordinate internally before giving an answer. Use the term for 'internal adjustment/coordination'.

modelAnswer.japanese: 社内調整してから後日ご連絡します。
modelAnswer.kana: しゃないちょうせいしてからごじつごれんらくします。
modelAnswer.english: I will contact you later after internal coordination.

stages.hint: 社内調整 (しゃないちょうせい) — internal coordination

stages.prompt: Someone just confirmed your request. Reply with a natural Japanese acknowledgment phrase thanking them for confirming.

modelAnswer.japanese: ご確認ありがとうございます。
modelAnswer.kana: ごかくにんありがとうございます。
modelAnswer.english: Thank you for confirming.

stages.hint: ご確認ありがとうございます — thank you for confirming

stages.prompt: Someone just contacted you. Reply with a natural acknowledgment phrase thanking them for contacting you.

modelAnswer.japanese: ご連絡ありがとうございます。承知しました。
modelAnswer.kana: ごれんらくありがとうございます。しょうちしました。
modelAnswer.english: Thank you for contacting me. Understood.

stages.hint: ご連絡ありがとうございます — thank you for contacting

packs.title: Presentations & Data
packs.titleJa: プレゼンとデータ分析
packs.description: Practice presentation vocabulary and data analysis terms

stages.prompt: You're preparing a presentation. Tell your team you'll share the slides.

modelAnswer.japanese: スライドを共有します。
modelAnswer.kana: スライドをきょうゆうします。
modelAnswer.english: I will share the slides.

stages.hint: スライド — slides

stages.prompt: You're presenting data. Tell your audience you've included reference materials.

modelAnswer.japanese: 参考資料も添付しています。
modelAnswer.kana: さんこうしりょうもてんぷしています。
modelAnswer.english: I've also attached reference materials.

stages.hint: 参考資料 (さんこうしりょう) — reference materials

stages.prompt: You're showing data visualization. Explain that this is a graph showing the trend.

modelAnswer.japanese: こちらのグラフは傾向を示しています。
modelAnswer.kana: こちらのグラフはけいこうをしめしています。
modelAnswer.english: This graph shows the trend.

stages.hint: グラフ — graph, 傾向 (けいこう) — trend

stages.prompt: You're presenting a data table. Tell the audience to please refer to the table.

modelAnswer.japanese: 表をご参照ください。
modelAnswer.kana: ひょうをごさんしょうください。
modelAnswer.english: Please refer to the table.

stages.hint: 表 (ひょう) — table

stages.prompt: You're analyzing two datasets. Explain that you're comparing the results.

modelAnswer.japanese: 2つのデータセットの結果を比較します。
modelAnswer.kana: ふたつのデータセットのけっかをひかくします。
modelAnswer.english: I will compare the results of the two datasets.

stages.hint: 比較する (ひかくする) — to compare

packs.title: Urgent Business Vocabulary
packs.description: Practice urgent requests and business context vocabulary

stages.prompt: You need something urgently. Ask your colleague to please handle this urgently.

modelAnswer.japanese: 至急対応をお願いします。
modelAnswer.kana: しきゅうたいおうをおねがいします。
modelAnswer.english: Please handle this urgently.

stages.hint: 至急 (しきゅう) — urgent, urgently

stages.prompt: You're explaining why you're developing a feature. Say it's based on actual demand.

modelAnswer.japanese: 実際の需要から開発しています。
modelAnswer.kana: じっさいのじゅようからかいはつしています。
modelAnswer.english: We're developing based on actual demand.

stages.hint: 需要 (じゅよう) — demand, need

stages.prompt: You're organizing a training session. Tell your team who can attend the training.

modelAnswer.japanese: 講習できる人が参加してください。
modelAnswer.kana: こうしゅうできるひとがさんかしてください。
modelAnswer.english: Please attend if you can take the training.

stages.hint: 講習 (こうしゅう) — training session

stages.prompt: You're presenting urgently needed data comparing demand trends. Use at least 3 presentation terms.

modelAnswer.japanese: 至急、需要の傾向を示すグラフを共有します。
modelAnswer.kana: しきゅう、じゅようのけいこうをしめすグラフをきょうゆうします。
modelAnswer.english: Urgently, I will share a graph showing demand trends.

stages.hint: 至急 + 需要 + 傾向 + グラフ

stages.prompt: You're hosting a training session on data analysis. Explain you'll compare tables and graphs showing trends.

modelAnswer.japanese: 講習では、傾向を示す表とグラフを比較します。
modelAnswer.kana: こうしゅうでは、けいこうをしめすひょうとグラフをひかくします。
modelAnswer.english: In the training session, we will compare tables and graphs showing trends.

stages.hint: 講習 + 表 + グラフ + 傾向 + 比較する
